articles.title: India slams Pakistan, calls out 'long-tainted genocidal record' at UNSC
articles.description: India called out Pakistan's "long-tainted" record of genocidal acts, telling the UN Security Council that the country's inhuman conduct reflects its attempts over decades to externalise internal failures through acts of violence within and beyond its border. Speaking at the Annual UNSC Open Debate on 'Protection of civilians in armed conflict' on Wednesday, India's Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni said, "It is ironic that Pakistan, with its long-tainted record of genocidal acts, has chosen to refer to issues that are strictly internal to India." Parvathaneni's remarks came after Pakistan's representative raised the issue of Jammu and Kashmir at the debate. Raising the issue of Pakistan's strikes on Afghanistan earlier this year, he said, "The world has not forgotten that it was during the holy month of Ramadan in March this year, at a time of peace, reflection, and mercy, that Pakistan conducted a barbaric airstrike on the Omid Addiction Treatment ..

articles.title: Agarkar's justification on Shami's snub called 'bakwaas', ex-IND batter asks 'would you treat Bumrah the same way?'
articles.description: Both team composition and injuries played a part in keeping Mohammed Shami out of reckoning for India’s series against Afghanistan that is scheduled for June.

articles.title: UP man strangles daughter over affair, carries semi-conscious body on bike to bury her
articles.description: Family members were allegedly against daughter's relationship with a young man from the same village, but Shivani had continued speaking to the youth over the phone for nearly a year.

articles.title: Punjab witnessing ‘pro-incumbency’ wave in favour of AAP, says Kejriwal
articles.description: Speaking to the media after he reached Ludhiana on a two-day visit to the state to hold meetings with the party leaders in preparation for the state assembly elections

articles.title: PM Modi To Chair High-Stakes Council Of Ministers’ Meet At Bharat Mandapam Today
articles.description: The Prime Minister will chair a meeting of the Council of Ministers at Bharat Mandapam during which he will review the government's performance, schemes, infrastructure projects.

articles.title: Falta repolling: Voting begins in West Bengal's high-stakes seat amid tight security
articles.description: Falta, which falls within Abhishek Banerjee’s bastion of Diamond Harbour, reflected the intense on-ground political heat during the West Bengal Assembly polls. Apart from authorities finding attempts to tamper with footage captured by web cameras installed at several polling stations, allegations of EVM manipulation were reported at a Falta booth, eventually prompting the Election Commission to order repolling.

articles.title: 3 weeks on, no breakthrough in first case registered under new anti-sacrilege Act
articles.description: The anti-sacrilege bill, which proposed amendments to the original 2008 Act, was passed unanimously by the Punjab Vidhan Sabha during a special one-day session on April 13

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan Monitors Social Media Crackdown, Orders Strict Vigilance Ahead Of NEET Re-Exam
articles.description: Dharmendra Pradhan directed authorities and social media platforms to crack down on fake NEET paper leak claims and online misinformation ahead of the June 21 re-examination.

articles.title: CJP overtakes BJP: ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ surpasses 9 million Instagram followers in just four days
articles.description: Discover how Abhijeet Dipke's satirical Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) crossed 9M Instagram followers, outgrowing major Indian political accounts in just 4 days.

articles.title: MLA Ayali quits SAD rebel faction, set to join Amritpal’s outfit
articles.description: Senior Akali leader and Dakha MLA Manpreet Singh Ayali on Wednesday resigned from the Shiromani Akali Dal breakaway faction SAD (Punar Surjit) and hinted at joining the Akali Dal Waris Punjab De, a fledgling political outfit headed by jailed Khadoor Sahib MP Amritpal Singh

articles.title: Tamil Nadu's Vijay-led TVK govt expands cabinet, 23 MLAs to take oath as ministers today
articles.description: Twenty-three newly elected MLAs will join the Tamil Nadu State Cabinet. Governor has approved their appointments. The swearing-in ceremony is scheduled for Thursday morning in Chennai. This expansion is the second major cabinet exercise by the Vijay-led TVK government. Several MLAs from various constituencies are set to take oath.

articles.title: ‘Justice Will Be Served’: White House Shares Post Claiming Trump ‘Neutralised’ America’s Enemies
articles.description: White House X post touts Trump neutralizing enemies, labels Maduro arrested, Khamenei and Abu Bilal al-Muniki killed, Raul Castro indicted, sparking online backlash over tone.

articles.title: Caste and crisis of fraternity
articles.description: The persistence of caste supremacy, to the extent of undermining the credibility and legitimacy of institutions, such as the Supreme Court and the office of the Chief Justice of India (CJI), is a serious concern. Such incidents are symptoms of deeply rooted caste-based discrimination and marginalisation.

articles.title: Dalit leaders divided over recording caste in census
articles.description: Grandsons of Dalit leader Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar are appealing to the community to mention their original caste in the caste column of the census form, while identifying themselves as ‘Buddhists’ in the column relating to religion. According to them, this would safeguard the social, political and financial benefits available to Scheduled Castes

articles.title: UP may keep village pradhans ‘in power’ even after their tenure
articles.description: Government is actively considering appointing village pradhans as administrators following the expiry of five-year term of existing gram panchayats on May 26

articles.title: Not the end for Mamata Banerjee, but the road back looks steep
articles.description: Not the end for Mamata, but the road back looks steep

articles.title: Work with resolve & efficiency to rid J&K of fear, terrorism: LG
articles.description: The lieutenant governor urged them to dedicate themselves to defending the unity and integrity of the motherland and to strengthen the proud legacy of this force

articles.title: Presidential Records Act ruling: Can Donald Trump ignore the Presidential Records Act? Judge says the White House must follow the law
articles.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to comply with the Presidential Records Act, rejecting a Justice Department opinion that deemed the law unconstitutional. U.S. District Judge John Bates ruled the act is likely constitutional and found a substantial risk of non-compliance.

articles.title: Aimee Bock led one of the largest frauds in history; she's blaming Minnesota for not stopping her
articles.description: Peel away all the political posturing, the thorny questions about Somali immigrants and the hundreds of millions of dollars in fraud in Minnesota, and you'll find Aimee Bock at the epicenter of things.

articles.title: India not a 'Dharamshala'; detect, delete, deport infiltrators: BJP
articles.description: Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar reiterated the Centre's "detect, delete and deport" policy on illegal infiltration, stating India is not a 'Dharamshala'. He said CAA is for refugees, but others will have to go back to their countries.

articles.title: LLB students protest after result
articles.description: Lucknow University LLB Semester-I students protest flawed results; varsity adopts NEP, passes all first-semester students and promotes odd-to-even semesters.

articles.description: Lawyers intensify stir, bar assns announce 6-day work boycott

articles.description: Chemists ride horse carts, e-rickshaws in Khargone to protest against online drug trade

articles.description: Commissioner inspects security arrangements at ghats

articles.title: Rai slams ‘Brahmanical patriarchy’ question
articles.description: UP Congress chief Ajai Rai urges BHU and other institutions stay free of BJP-RSS interference after row over “Brahmanical patriarchy” exam question.

articles.description: Interim relief for candidates who challenged SI re-exam eligibility

articles.description: Govt hands over 27-km stretch to BSF for border fencing

articles.title: MLA alleges conspiracy over husband’s ‘fake’ poll nomination
articles.description: MLA alleges conspiracy over husband’s ‘fake’ poll nomination

articles.title: TDP to introduce 19 resolutions during two-day hybrid Mahanadu from May 27
articles.description: TDP's Mahanadu on May 27-28 will introduce 19 resolutions, broadcast live to state clusters, says MLA Somireddy Chandramohan Reddy.

articles.title: Rubio offers Cubans 'new path' as US signals tougher stance on Cuba leadership
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio urged Cubans to embrace a “new path” and criticised Cuba’s leadership, as reports suggest Washington may indict former president Raúl Castro amid rising tensions.

articles.title: India can help bring peace to Europe, says Estonia's Foreign Minister
articles.description: Estonia's Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna praised growing India-Estonia ties in tech and AI. He urged India to use its global influence to apply diplomatic pressure on Russia to help bring peace to Europe amid the ongoing Ukraine conflict.

articles.title: Delhi HC seeks response on PIL for CBI probe into fake universities
articles.description: The Delhi High Court sought responses from the Centre, UGC, and others on a PIL demanding a CBI probe into fake universities allegedly issuing invalid degrees. A bench noted the 'serious issues' raised concerning students and the education system.

articles.title: Three-language policy: Why Tamil Nadu sees an old threat in the new CBSE circular
articles.description: India's three-language policy is back at the centre of national debate, and the argument is the same one it has always been. The CBSE's 2026 mandate to make three languages compulsory up to Class 10 is framed as a step towards multilingualism and national integration. In Tamil Nadu and much of the South, it is being read as something else entirely. The question is not really about languages. It never was.

articles.title: AAP to 'break its own record' in Punjab polls, says Arvind Kejriwal
articles.description: AAP National Convenor Arvind Kejriwal expressed confidence in the party breaking its own record in Punjab's upcoming elections. Citing a 'pro-incumbency' wave, he credited the developmental work of the Bhagwant Mann-led government for the positive sentiment.

articles.title: Exclusive | Swedish Prime Minister Kristersson: India-EU Ties Not Living Up To Full Potential Yet
articles.description: PM Narendra Modi and Swedish PM Ulf Kristersson elevated ties to a strategic partnership in Gothenburg, targeting doubled trade and investment within five years

articles.title: Italy PM Giorgia Meloni Speaks Hindi During Joint Presser With PM Modi | Viral ‘Melodi’ Moment
articles.description: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni surprised many by speaking in Hindi during a joint press conference with PM Narendra Modi in Rome. Her remark quickly went viral online as India and Italy announced a major upgrade in bilateral ties, elevating relations to a “Special Strategic Partnership.”

articles.title: Simultaneous polls can save ₹7 lakh crore: BJP MP Chaudhary
articles.description: Joint Parliamentary panel’s recommendations propose synchronising Lok Sabha, Assembly elections in 1st phase, followed by Panchayat, Municipality polls within 100 days of general elections

articles.title: Chinese Proverb of the Day: “Women hold up half the... — Life lessons on empowerment shared responsibility, social progress and why equality benefits everyone
articles.description: Chinese Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “Women hold up half the sky.” The saying emphasizes the essential role women play in society, family life, leadership, and progress. It reflects the belief that the world cannot function or advance without the contributions, strength, and efforts of women. The proverb also promotes the idea of equality, suggesting that women deserve the same recognition, opportunities, and respect as men in every aspect of life.

articles.title: 'What is wrong with it?' On caste census, SC says govt should know number of people in backward class
articles.description: The Supreme Court has upheld the Centre's decision to conduct caste enumeration in the upcoming 2027 Census, stating there is "nothing wrong with it." A bench led by the Chief Justice emphasized that such data is crucial for the government to identify backward classes and implement effective welfare measures. This marks the first comprehensive caste count since 1931.

articles.title: War Will 'Spread Far Beyond Region' If US, Israel Resume Attacks: Iran Guards
articles.description: The statement comes after remarks by US President Donald Trump, who said Washington could strike Iran again if the two sides fail to reach a lasting settlement in the coming days.

articles.title: Politicising Zubeen Garg's death backfired on Congress-led opposition in Assam polls: Himanta Biswa Sarma
articles.description: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma visited Zubeen Garg's cremation site, asserting the Congress-led opposition paid for politicizing the musician's death during polls. He emphasized Garg as an emotion, not a political tool, and reviewed development work at 'Zubeen Khetra' as a tribute to the beloved artist. The opposition had criticized the government over alleged delays in justice for Garg.

articles.title: IT employees feel the squeeze as firms redraw salary structures under labour-code shift
articles.description: Compensation experts, staffing firms, and labour lawyers told Moneycontrol that the changes are not isolated to TCS and reflect a broader shift underway across India’s IT and corporate sector as firms prepare for implementation of the new labour codes.

articles.title: Your Kundli May Already Know the Exact Date You’ll Get a Government Job | Ft. Pawan Sinha
articles.description: Can astrology really predict your chances of getting a government job? In this discussion, Pawan Sinha explains how kundli combinations, planetary periods, and career houses may influence success in competitive exams and stable government careers. Why do some people face repeated delays despite years of preparation? Could your birth chart reveal the right timing for career breakthroughs and exam success? This conversation explores government job yog, astrology predictions, career timing, and kundli analysis for aspirants preparing for government exams. Watch till the end for deeper insights.#timesmantra #governmentjob #kundli #astrology #govtexam #careersuccess #pawnsinha #jobprediction #horoscope #upsc #ssc #indiastrology

articles.title: US pressure, Imran Khan’s ouster and Pakistan’s nuclear secrets: What the leaked cypher reveals
articles.description: The diplomatic cable published by independent news outlet Drop Site has revived Khan’s long-standing claims of a foreign-backed conspiracy, once again placing Pakistan’s fragile civil-military balance under scrutiny

articles.title: Chief minister Dhami pays heartfelt tribute to General Khanduri
articles.description: Chief minister Dhami pays heartfelt tribute to General Khanduri

articles.title: Trump hits pause as Gulf monarchies fear Iran’s retaliation
articles.description: The Strait of Hormuz has now become less a waterway and more a loaded gun placed on the table. Legally, Iran does not own it. Militarily, however, Iran has spent decades ensuring that nobody can use it peacefully without accounting for Tehran’s presence

articles.title: Who is Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, US and Israel's pick to lead Iran?
articles.description: At the outset of the Iran war, US President Donald Trump and Israel declared ‘regime change’ as the goal. Now, it has been revealed that they wanted Mahmoud Ahmadinejad to lead the country. Ironically, during his time in office, the former president was widely derided in the West for his anti-American and anti-Israeli stance

articles.title: China’s Xi Welcomes Putin ‘Like A King’, Takes 'Veiled Dig' At U.S. After Trump Visit | Watch
articles.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised China-Russia ties as a force of “calm amid chaos” during high-profile talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing. The meeting came just days after Xi hosted President Donald Trump for a major U.S.-China summit, with analysts pointing to Xi’s remarks as a veiled swipe at Washington’s global influence. As tensions between China, Russia, and the West continue to grow, both leaders projected unity and deepening strategic coordination in the face of what Beijing called “unilateral hegemonic currents.”
